title: Rate-Limit Requests in Nginx
description:
## Protect a Backend From Abuse

**Your task:** write a complete Nginx config at **`/root/rl.conf`** that:
1. defines a rate-limit zone named **`mylimit`** keyed by client IP (`limit_req_zone $binary_remote_addr ...`) at **`10r/s`**
2. **applies** that zone (`limit_req zone=mylimit ...`) inside a `location`
3. passes **`nginx -t`**

> 💡 `limit_req_zone` is declared in the `http { }` context; `limit_req` is used in a `location`. Test with `nginx -t -c /root/rl.conf`.

hints.title: Define then apply the zone
hints.body: Declare the shared-memory zone with a rate in http{}, then reference it in a location with limit_req (burst smooths spikes).

hints.title: Validate
hints.body: nginx -t checks the syntax and the directive contexts.

validation.description: Checks valid config, a 10r/s zone named mylimit, and that it is applied.
